raw-backfill: example ingest. The select_raw_batch.py digest above lists unprocessed raw files. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call (file_read on the first raw file, or $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md for the workflow). For each file, run the §Ingest workflow in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md: create a scored source page keyed by `raw:`; extract entities worth tracking; cross-link. File a prediction only for an explicit, falsifiable, dated claim. WRITE via the MCP write path (mcp_okengine_write_create_entity / update_entity), NOT file_write; never write findings/ (human-only). LOCAL-ONLY: do not call web_search/web_extract/web_crawl (shared paid budget). Append one wiki/log.md summary line.
entity-backfill: example entity backfill. The select_entity_candidates.py digest above lists existing entity pages + recent sources. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create or update entity pages for subjects worth tracking over time (competitors, products, segments, deals); dedupe against existing; cross-link. WRITE via the MCP write path; for LIST fields like `sources:` send the COMPLETE updated list (read first, append, send all). Do NOT create concepts or predictions here. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
concept-backfill: example concept backfill. The select_concept_candidates.py digest above lists missing concept slugs (wikilinked but no page). FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create concept pages (a cross-cutting pattern / segment / cluster) synthesized from the citing sources. If a slug is really an entity name, it is an ENTITY not a concept — skip and note it. WRITE via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
source-quality-backfill: Source scoring. The select_unscored_sources.py digest above lists source pages lacking reliability and credibility. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the rubric in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, set reliability and credibility for each by UPDATING FRONTMATTER ONLY via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (pass frontmatter_yaml, NO body — the body is preserved). LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
schema-classify-drain: Type classification. The select_schema_classify.py digest above lists entities typed `entity`/`organization` that need a canonical type. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md taxonomy, set `type:` to the single best canonical type via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (frontmatter merge) — or leave the page alone if no canonical type honestly fits. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
page-quality-enrich: Deepen thin pages. The select_page_quality_enrich.py digest above lists deficient entity/concept pages with the source pages that cite each. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, AUGMENT each target — append 2-4 `##` sections of real analysis grounded ONLY in the citing sources — via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section. Never replace existing content. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
daily-brief:
Daily competitive brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (the derived load-first set: recent sources, recently-updated entities, open predictions). Then write today's brief via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity to path "briefings/<YYYY-MM-DD>" (the enforced write path — never file_write) (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD> — published drives tier recency) summarizing the 3-5 most significant developments, each 2-3 sentences with [[wikilinks]] to the entities + their source. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

The select_daily_brief.py digest above lists the window's fresh sources, entity movement, due predictions, and new gaps — TRUST THE DIGEST; do not tour the vault.
